'''Michael "Bruno" Lawrence''' (born 12 April 1990) is a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|lf}} for the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[RFL Championship]]. He was both an [[England national rugby league team|England Knights]] and [[Jamaica national rugby league team|Jamaican]] international,<ref name="England Knights v Jamaica">{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/england-knights-v-jamaica-international-2019/719012#lineup|title=England Knights v Jamaica|publisher=WWOS|date=20 October 2019|accessdate=25 October 2019}}</ref> and has played as a {{rlp|ce|pr|wg}}er and {{rlp|sr}} forward at various stages during his career.

==Background==
Lawrence was born in [[Huddersfield]], West Yorkshire, England and has Jamaican heritage. Lawrence is a product of the Giants' academy system.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.giantsrl.com/locker-room/profile/388/michael-lawrence|title = Huddersfield Giants - Rugby Super League}}</ref> and represented the [[England Knights]] in the [[2012 European Cup (rugby league)|European cup in 2012]]<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/news/huddersfield-giants-michael-lawrence-impresses-4941607|title = Huddersfield Giants' Michael Lawrence impresses as England Knights put Ireland to the sword|date = 22 October 2012}}</ref>
In 2019 he enjoyed a well earned [[Testimonial match|Testimonial]] year. He was a firm favourite amongst the Huddersfield supporters who referred to him as "Bruno", a nickname due to his likeness to the famous British boxer [[Frank Bruno]].

==Career==
It was announced in July 2007 that Lawrence would become a member Huddersfield's [[Super League]] squad for the 2008 season.<ref>{{cite web|title=Lawrence in Super Call| url=http://www.examiner.co.uk/huddersfield-giants/huddersfield-giants-news/tm_method=full&objectid=19404053&siteid=50060-name_page.html|author=Chris Roberts, Hudd Main|date=4 July 2007|work=examiner.co.uk}}</ref> Lawrence's previous club was [[Newsome Panthers]].
Lawrence was selected for the England Academy team in the 2007 season, a reward for consistently good performances throughout the season with Huddersfield's Academy.<ref>{{cite web|title=Michael handed super call| url=http://www.examiner.co.uk/huddersfield-giants/huddersfield-giants-news/tm_headline=michael-handed-gb-call&method=full&objectid=19676208&siteid=50060-name_page.html|author=Chris Roberts|date=23 August 2007|work=examiner.co.uk }}</ref>
Lawrence made his [[Super League]] début during Huddersfield's important fixture with [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] on 31 August 2007. He started the game on the substitutes' bench and replaced [[Chris Nero]] for the last five minutes of the game. This made him the first player born in the 1990s to play super league. 

Since then Lawrence enjoyed a trip to the [[Challenge Cup]] final at Wembley in 2009 and was part of the history making squad that lifted the league leaders shield in 2013. Lawrence was granted a year long testimonial in 2019 for over ten years loyal service to the Huddersfield club. In 2012, Lawrence was a part of the England Knights team that won the European cup, and again in 2013 when they beat Samoa. In 2019, Lawrence represented Jamaica in a full international, ironically against the England knights at Leeds.{{cn|date=October 2022}}

After 324 games for the Huddersfield club, Lawrence signed a contract to join RFL Championship side [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]] for the 2023 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/that-didnt-sit-right-with-me-michael-lawrence-explains-why-he-swapped-huddersfield-giants-for-bradford-bulls-4012250/|title='That didn't sit right with me': Michael Lawrence explains why he swapped Huddersfield Giants for Bradford Bulls|website=www.yorkshirepost.co.uk}}</ref>

On 29 September 2025 he announced that he had retired from the professional game<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=29 Sep 2025|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Huddersfield legend announces retirement as Giants pay tribute to ‘true great’|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/michael-lawrence-announces-retirement}}</ref>
